title: cracca longipes
intro: Cracca longipes, also known as the 'Crested Winter-vetch', is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is a perennial climbing legume native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Cracca longipes include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, and it can be prepared as a herbal infusion, tincture, or poultice for topical application.
